TerminalInput.description: A dark, immersive code editor-like input area. Syntax highlighting is key.
TerminalInput.styles: bg-[#020617] text-green-400 font-mono p-6 rounded-xl border border-white/5 shadow-inner min-h-[300px] focus-within:ring-1 focus-within:ring-primary/50

TranslationPanel.description: The 'human' side. Friendly, readable, explaining the code.
TranslationPanel.styles: bg-slate-900/50 backdrop-blur-md border border-white/10 p-6 rounded-xl shadow-lg h-full overflow-y-auto

GitAnalyzer.description: Visual dashboard for repo stats. Uses Bento grid.

StatCard.styles: bg-slate-800/50 border border-white/5 p-4 rounded-lg hover:bg-slate-800 transition-colors group relative overflow-hidden

ModeToggle.styles: flex items-center space-x-2 bg-slate-900/80 p-1 rounded-full border border-white/10

setup: Install required packages: `yarn add framer-motion react-syntax-highlighter react-markdown clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react`
Configure Tailwind with the specified colors and fonts in `tailwind.config.js`.
Add 'Space Grotesk' and 'Manrope' via Google Fonts in `index.css` or `index.html`.
implementation_steps: 1. Setup the global layout with a sidebar/navbar and the main content area.
2. Create the `TerminalInput` component using a textarea or code editor library, styled to look like a terminal.
3. Create the `TranslationPanel` with a clean, readable typography for the AI output.
4. Implement the 'Git Analyzer' view using a Bento grid layout for stats.
5. Use `framer-motion` for smooth transitions between tabs and for entry animations of cards.
6. Ensure the 'Mode Toggle' (Beginner/Familiar) is prominent and functional.
critical_constraints: Do NOT use pure black (#000000). Use Slate-950 (#020617) for the darkest elements.
Ensure text contrast is high (WCAG AA).
Use 'Glassmorphism' sparingly - mainly for the translation panel to differentiate it from the raw terminal.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
